node.title: Snapchat Uber | Minimum time to complete all tasks

topic.title: Snapchat Uber | Minimum time to complete all tasks

post.content:
I was asked the same question in Snapchat phone screen as well as uber onsite. The question goes as follows.

There are some tasks/microservices n1, n2, n3, n4 etc.  You will be given a dependency list for eg
[(n1, n2), (n2, n3)] signifying n1 depends on n2 and n2 depends on n3 and so on.

Every task/microservice needs time to execute. The time for task/microservice is t1, t2, t3, t3 for n1, n2, n3 and n4 respectively.

The task is to find the minimum time to complete all tasks.


I used topological sort to solve it but apparently it was not the best possible answer.
My thought is that we just need to build the graph and record all the indegree 0 node and DFS find the longest time. This is based on the unlimited processors

find all the different groups of tasks, in a group of tasks the time taken will be summed up. Now the maximum sum will be the minimum time to complete all the tasks. As all the groups of microservice can operate in parallel.

I believe, you started well but the problem is what if you have two independent task groups. or even this: `n1-n2, n1-n3 (ex 1)`.
ex 1: n2 and n3 can run simultaneously followed by n1. So total time being `Math.max(t2, t3) + t1`.

Same holds true for completely independent groups. ex 2: `n1-n2, n2-n3, n4-n5, n5-n6`
ex 2: ```Math.max(t1 + t2 + t3, t4 + t5 + t6)```
